message:
fix(windows): enable ANSI escape sequence support on Windows CMD.exe (#47)

Windows CMD.exe does not process ANSI escape sequences by default,
causing colored output to render as garbage (e.g. "←[92m"). This adds
an early call to SetConsoleMode with ENABLE_VIRTUAL_TERMINAL_PROCESSING
via the `enable-ansi-support` crate, gated behind #[cfg(windows)].
Failures are silently ignored to avoid crashing on older Windows versions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
